what_it_does: Discovers every registered role via RoleRegistry, walks each role's output_schema and every nested Pydantic sub-model, and fails loudly if any field is named confidence (case-insensitive), enforcing a no-confidence-field policy.
observations: Docstring frames the test as G16 / Tim's standing law: 'no confidence, tags and counts' and 'enforce, don't convention'
Uses RoleRegistry().discover(['roles']) as the source of truth and skips roles with no output_schema
Recursively inspects Pydantic BaseModel fields using typing.get_args to follow list[X], Optional[X], dict[_,X], etc.
Detects confidence case-insensitively and reports dotted field paths per role
Maintains a global PASS counter and uses a custom check() helper that asserts and prints
standouts: Treats a design principle as an automated, fail-fast registry-boundary invariant
Walks nested Pydantic models generically rather than only top-level schemas
Registry-is-truth: adding a role with a confidence field automatically turns the suite red
conventions: Inserts project root into sys.path and calls os.chdir(ROOT) at module import time
Uses noqa: E402 on imports that follow path manipulation
Nests recursive helper functions (rec, walk) inside their callers
Sorts role iteration for deterministic output
Docstring doubles as a policy manifest
concerns: Mutates global sys.path and os.chdir at import time, affecting the importing environment
Relies on a mutable global PASS counter rather than a standard test runner idiom
Nested walkers capture and mutate enclosing out/seen lists
Case-insensitive 'confidence' match could flag fields like 'confidence_interval' if they existed
No explicit handling of Pydantic aliases, computed fields, or non-BaseModel output_schema types
notes: A policy-as-code invariant test that bans confidence fields from registered role output schemas by recursively walking Pydantic models.
questions: What is G16 and who/what authority does 'Tim' represent?
How does RoleRegistry discover roles and guarantee completeness?
Are output_schema values always Pydantic BaseModel subclasses?
Should fields such as 'confidence_score' or 'confidence_interval' also be banned, or only exact 'confidence'?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it walks registry role output schemas and nested models and asserts no confidence fields exist.
apparent_themes: Evidence as tags and counts, not probability floats
intention_signals: The file is trying to institutionalize a design rule by making it an automatic, fail-fast gate at the role registry boundary so new roles cannot silently ship confidence fields.
novelty: A runtime registry scan that recursively inspects Pydantic output schemas to enforce a naming ban, elevating a design principle into a standing invariant test.
contribution: Provides an automated, deterministic enforcement point for the no-confidence-field rule across all registered role output schemas, including nested sub-models, and surfaces violations with role IDs and dotted field paths.
summary_for_embedding: A Pydantic-schema invariant test that discovers every registered role's output_schema and recursively fails if any field is named confidence, enforcing a tags-and-counts policy.
intention_for_embedding: To make the 'no confidence floats, only tags and counts' design law a standing, registry-boundary invariant rather than a manual convention.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that fails with a message or increments a global pass counter and prints ok.
symbols.description: check(msg, cond) asserts the supplied condition; on failure it raises with 'FAIL: {msg}', on success it increments the global PASS variable and prints an ok line.

symbols.does: Extracts all BaseModel subclasses reachable from a type annotation.
symbols.description: _models_in(annotation) returns a list of Pydantic BaseModel subclasses found by recursively traversing the type arguments of the given annotation, handling generics such as list[X], Optional[X], and dict[_, X].

symbols.does: Nested recursive worker that walks type arguments to collect BaseModel subclasses.
symbols.description: rec(a) is defined inside _models_in; if a is a BaseModel subclass it is appended to the enclosing out list, otherwise the function recurses over get_args(a).

symbols.does: Finds every field path named confidence in a Pydantic model and its nested sub-models.
symbols.description: confidence_fields(schema) walks the supplied model and all reachable BaseModel sub-models, collecting dotted field paths whose name equals 'confidence' case-insensitively, while avoiding revisiting already-seen models.

symbols.does: Nested recursive worker that visits model fields and accumulates confidence-named field paths.
symbols.description: walk(model, prefix) is defined inside confidence_fields; it marks models as seen, iterates over model_fields, records any field named confidence (case-insensitive), and recurses into nested models discovered via _models_in.
